# dokploy_compose (Resource)

A Docker Compose stack managed by Dokploy. v0.5 supports source_type "raw" only (paste the YAML inline).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_compose" "monitoring" {
  environment_id = dokploy_project.obs.production_environment_id
  name           = "monitoring"

  compose_file = <<-EOT
    version: "3.8"
    services:
      prometheus:
        image: prom/prometheus:latest
        restart: unless-stopped
  EOT

  env = {
    PROM_PORT = "9090"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `compose_file` (String) Contents of the docker-compose.yml file.
- `environment_id` (String) Environment that owns the stack. Changing forces replacement.
- `name` (String) Display name.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description. Once set, removing this attribute does not clear it on the server.
- `env` (Map of String) Env vars passed to the stack.
- `server_id` (String) Managed server ID. Omit to run on the Dokploy host. Changing forces replacement.
- `source_type` (String) Source type. v0.5 only supports `raw` (inline YAML).
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `app_name` (String) Internal name generated by Dokploy.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (String) Status of the most recent deploy.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_compose.monitoring <composeId>
```
